choice_context: A person is deciding which movie to watch based on the movie's description, genre, cast, and plot summary.

dimensions.name: Action Intensity

low_pole.label: Low Action
low_pole.description: Movies with minimal action sequences and focus more on dialogue and character development.
low_pole.typical_person: People who prefer quieter, more introspective films.

high_pole.label: High Action
high_pole.description: Movies filled with intense action scenes, stunts, and fast-paced sequences.

dimensions.scoring_guidance: Score based on the frequency and intensity of action scenes.

dimensions.name: Emotional Depth

low_pole.label: Shallow Emotions
low_pole.description: Films that do not delve deeply into characters' emotional states or relationships.
low_pole.typical_person: Viewers who prefer straightforward narratives without complex emotional layers.

high_pole.label: Deep Emotions
high_pole.description: Films that explore complex emotional landscapes and deep character relationships.
high_pole.typical_person: Individuals who appreciate films that evoke strong emotional responses.

dimensions.scoring_guidance: Rate based on the depth and complexity of emotional themes explored.

dimensions.name: Sci-Fi Elements

low_pole.label: Minimal Sci-Fi
low_pole.description: Movies that have little to no science fiction elements, focusing instead on realistic settings.
low_pole.typical_person: Audiences who prefer grounded, non-fantastical stories.

high_pole.label: Heavy Sci-Fi
high_pole.description: Films rich in futuristic technology, space travel, and speculative science concepts.
high_pole.typical_person: Enthusiasts of science fiction and speculative storytelling.

dimensions.scoring_guidance: Score according to the prominence and integration of sci-fi elements.

dimensions.name: Comedy Level

low_pole.label: Little Comedy
low_pole.description: Movies that are serious in tone with few comedic moments.
low_pole.typical_person: People who prefer drama and seriousness over humor.

high_pole.label: High Comedy
high_pole.description: Films that are primarily focused on humor and light-heartedness.
high_pole.typical_person: Fans of comedy who seek laughter and entertainment.

dimensions.scoring_guidance: Rate based on the amount and effectiveness of comedic content.

dimensions.name: Historical Accuracy

low_pole.label: Loose History
low_pole.description: Films that take significant liberties with historical facts and timelines.
low_pole.typical_person: Viewers who prioritize story over strict adherence to history.

high_pole.label: Strict History
high_pole.description: Movies that meticulously adhere to historical events and details.
high_pole.typical_person: Historians and history buffs who value accuracy.

dimensions.scoring_guidance: Score based on how closely the film adheres to known historical facts.

dimensions.name: Visual Effects

low_pole.label: Minimal VFX
low_pole.description: Movies that use very few visual effects, relying more on practical effects and real locations.
low_pole.typical_person: Audiences who prefer authenticity and dislike digital alterations.

high_pole.label: Heavy VFX
high_pole.description: Films that extensively use computer-generated imagery and special effects.
high_pole.typical_person: Fans of visually stunning and fantastical worlds.

dimensions.scoring_guidance: Rate based on the quantity and quality of visual effects used.

dimensions.name: Character Complexity

low_pole.label: Simple Characters
low_pole.description: Films featuring characters with straightforward motivations and personalities.
low_pole.typical_person: Viewers who prefer clear-cut heroes and villains.

high_pole.label: Complex Characters
high_pole.description: Movies with multi-dimensional characters whose motivations and personalities evolve.
high_pole.typical_person: Individuals who enjoy nuanced and evolving character arcs.

dimensions.scoring_guidance: Score based on the depth and evolution of character development.

dimensions.name: Dialogue Quality

low_pole.label: Weak Dialogue
low_pole.description: Movies with poorly written or uninteresting dialogue.
low_pole.typical_person: People who may overlook dialogue if other aspects like visuals are strong.

high_pole.label: Strong Dialogue
high_pole.description: Films with well-crafted, engaging, and meaningful dialogue.
high_pole.typical_person: Critics and viewers who appreciate sharp, intelligent writing.

dimensions.scoring_guidance: Rate based on the quality and impact of the dialogue.

dimensions.name: Music Influence

low_pole.label: Background Music
low_pole.description: Films where music plays a minor role, serving only as background.
low_pole.typical_person: Viewers who are less sensitive to the musical score.

high_pole.label: Central Music
high_pole.description: Movies where the music is integral to the narrative and emotional experience.
high_pole.typical_person: Individuals who appreciate films where the soundtrack significantly enhances the story.

dimensions.scoring_guidance: Score based on the importance and integration of music within the film.

dimensions.name: Pacing Speed

low_pole.label: Slow Pacing
low_pole.description: Movies that move at a leisurely pace, allowing time for detailed exploration of scenes and characters.
low_pole.typical_person: Audiences who prefer thoughtful, unhurried storytelling.

high_pole.label: Fast Pacing
high_pole.description: Films that maintain a quick pace, often with rapid scene transitions and action.

dimensions.scoring_guidance: Rate based on the overall speed and rhythm of the film's pacing.
